titleStmt.title: Letter from Arthur Megaw to Edward O'Farrell, 22 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Arthur Megaw

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from solicitor Arthur Megaw (b.1873) to Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), Assistant under-secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne (1873-1939). The letter thanks the assistant under-secretary for sending him a conformation telegram informing him that his last letter was received.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO RP/1916/8719/2

correspAction.placeName: 22 Ocean Buildings, Donegall Square East, Belfast, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
22, Ocean Buildings,
Donegall Square East,
Belfast. 22nd May, 1916. Sir,
Government Scheme re claims in respect
of civil liabilities of soldiers I am much obliged for your telegram
of 20th inst received this morning and hope
you will be kind enough to let me know should
you hear anything as to the proposed arrange-
ments for Ireland or what Authority will make same Your obedient servant Arthur Megaw The Assistant Under Secretary for Ireland
Dublin Castle
